data.narrative:
Slingshot Aerospace is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $120M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage peers in the same sector, including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Stoke Space, and Aalyria.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $120M total raised proxy, implying a $240M valuation. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $400M median, with a range of $274M (Sift, Series B) to $1.3B (Aalyria, Series B). Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, though the sector median trades at 45.3x P/S across the public set. Public-comp anchoring is excluded from triangulation accordingly.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $204M to $460M, with a median implied value of $320M. This range reflects material dispersion in peer valuations—a 4.7x spread between Series B outliers—and uncertainty around Slingshot's relative market position within the frontier aerospace stack. Stage progression suggests the lower end; peer medians suggest the upper half. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, technical execution risk, and whether Series B momentum in the sector will sustain.
